title: Universal Portfolio Shrinkage Approximation
abstract: Example generated by Copilot GPT-5.4 for the Kelly, Malamud, Pourmohammadi, and Trojani (2025) UPSA paper2code workflow.
methodology: UPSA constructs a nonlinear spectral shrinkage portfolio as a non-negative ensemble of ridge portfolios. It estimates an uncentered rolling second-moment matrix, computes ridge portfolios over a fixed penalty grid, estimates ridge-portfolio moments with leave-one-out cross-validation, solves a non-negative Markowitz problem in ridge space, and back-projects the ensemble into factor-portfolio weights.
data_description: Monthly JKP factor portfolio returns in wide form: one date column and one return column per factor. Optional reference weights are keyed by fit_date for validation.
signal_logic: This is a monthly portfolio-construction strategy over factor returns, not a price-trigger trading rule. Weights estimated at the end of month t are applied to factor returns in month t+1.
formulas: Ridge weights: pi_z = (Sigma_bar + z I)^(-1) mu_bar
UPSA ensemble: non-negative weights over ridge portfolios
One-period lag: weights fit at t apply to returns at t+1
references: Kelly, Malamud, Pourmohammadi, and Trojani (2025), NBER
full_text: UPSA paper2code example generated by Copilot GPT-5.4. The full paper text is not embedded in this compact example artifact.
